payload.text: Getting enough deep sleep is one of the simplest ways to protect your health. While we rest, the brain quietly {{b1}} the memories we formed during the day and clears away waste that builds up in its cells. People who sleep too little often struggle to {{b2}} on simple tasks and feel far more stressed than they need to.

payload.text: Deep beneath the waves lies a world that remains almost entirely unexplored. Because sunlight cannot {{b1}} the darkest depths, scientists rely on robots and powerful lights to study creatures that have never seen the surface. Each dive tends to {{b2}} new species, reminding us how little of our own planet we truly understand.

payload.text: Volunteering can change a community in ways that money alone cannot. When neighbours give their time to clean a park or {{b1}} an elderly person with the shopping, they build trust that lasts long after the task is done. Small acts of kindness often {{b2}} others to join in, and slowly a stronger, friendlier place begins to grow.

payload.text: The coffee in your morning cup travels a surprisingly long way before it reaches you. Farmers in warm, hilly regions carefully {{b1}} the ripe red cherries by hand, often high on steep slopes. After the beans are dried and roasted, their rich flavour depends heavily on how the plants were {{b2}} throughout the growing season.

gaps.before: The results were far from certain;
gaps.after: , the team decided to publish them and let others test the claims.
gaps.explanation: 「結果は確実とは言えなかった → それでも公表した」という譲歩の関係。even so（それでもなお）が前後の逆接をつなぐ。as a result は因果で不自然。

gaps.before: Had the warning been issued earlier,
gaps.explanation: Had + 主語 + 過去分詞 は仮定法過去完了の倒置形（＝If the warning had been issued earlier）。帰結節は would/could have + 過去分詞。よって could have reduced が正しい。

gaps.after: a link between air quality and sleep.
gaps.explanation: establish a link で「関連を明らかにする・立証する」という定番のコロケーション。link と自然に結びつく動詞は establish。

readingQuestions.prompt: 文脈に最も合う選択肢を選んでください。

gaps.after: the habit of checking her phone first thing every morning.
gaps.explanation: break a habit で「習慣をやめる・断つ」という定番の表現。habit と結びつく動詞は break。

gaps.after: more time yesterday, I would have finished the report.
gaps.explanation: 過去の事実に反する仮定（仮定法過去完了）なので if節は had + 過去分詞。have の過去分詞は had なので had had が正しい。

gaps.after: attention to the new safety rules.
gaps.explanation: pay attention to ~ で「～に注意を払う」。attention と結びつく動詞は pay。

readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: The Quiet Power of Small Habits
paragraphs: When people decide to change their lives, they often dream of dramatic action: a strict new diet, an hour of exercise every dawn, a promise to read a book a week. Such grand plans feel exciting for a few days, yet most of them collapse within a month. The reason is not weak character. It is that huge changes demand huge effort, and effort is exactly the thing that runs out first.

Researchers who study behaviour suggest a gentler path. Instead of trying to remake themselves overnight, people do far better by attaching a tiny new action to something they already do. Reading a single page after brushing your teeth, or doing two push-ups before your morning coffee, feels almost too small to matter. Precisely because it is so small, it is easy to repeat, and repetition is where real change hides.

Over months, these tiny actions grow quietly, like interest building in a bank account. One page becomes a chapter; two push-ups become twenty. The habit no longer relies on willpower, because it has become part of who you are. The lesson is not that ambition is wrong, but that lasting change is usually built from steps so small they are almost invisible.

questions.prompt: 本文の主旨として最も適切なものはどれですか。
options: Lasting change usually comes from tiny, repeatable habits rather than sudden, dramatic effort.
People should always set the most ambitious goals they possibly can.
Willpower is the only thing that decides whether a habit survives.
Exercise and reading are the only habits worth building.

questions.before: According to the writer, grand plans often fail because huge changes demand huge

questions.prompt: 小さな習慣が時間とともに成長する様子をたとえている語句はどれですか。

spans.text: a strict new diet, an hour of exercise every dawn
most of them collapse within a month
these tiny actions grow quietly, like interest building in a bank account
effort is exactly the thing that runs out first

questions.sentence: The trick, they argue, is to make the first step so easy that failing to do it feels harder than doing it.

questions.prompt: 筆者が最後の段落で最も伝えたいことはどれですか。
options: Real, lasting change is built from steps so small they are almost invisible, until the habit becomes part of who you are.
Ambition is always a mistake and should be avoided completely.
Habits can only be formed by using strong willpower every single day.
Saving money in a bank is the best way to change your behaviour.

readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: The City That Turned Off Its Lights
paragraphs: For most of human history, the night sky was a familiar sight. People could look up almost anywhere and see thousands of stars, along with the pale band of the Milky Way. Today, however, the glow of streetlights, offices, and advertising signs has washed much of that view away. In many large cities, a child may grow up able to count the visible stars on two hands.

This constant brightness, known as light pollution, does more than hide the stars. Bright nights confuse birds that travel by the moon, keep insects circling uselessly around lamps, and disturb the sleep of people living nearby. Wasted light also means wasted energy, as electricity pours upward into an empty sky instead of lighting the streets below.

Some towns have decided to fight back. By using warmer, dimmer lamps that point downward, and by switching off unnecessary lights late at night, they have cut their energy bills and brought a little darkness back. In a few places, residents have gathered in parks to watch the stars reappear, rediscovering a view their grandparents once took for granted.

questions.prompt: 本文が最も伝えたいことはどれですか。
options: Light pollution hides the stars and causes other harm, but careful changes can bring the night sky back.
City lights have no effect on animals or on people's sleep.
The Milky Way has completely disappeared from the sky forever.
Turning off streetlights always makes cities more dangerous.

questions.before: The writer says that bright nights can confuse birds that travel by the

questions.prompt: 光害を減らすために町が実際に取った行動を述べている語句はどれですか。

spans.text: a child may grow up able to count the visible stars on two hands
using warmer, dimmer lamps that point downward
the glow of streetlights, offices, and advertising signs has washed much of that view away
keep insects circling uselessly around lamps

questions.prompt: 本文全体の要点はどれですか。
options: Light pollution harms wildlife, sleep, and energy use, yet simple steps can restore darker, starrier nights.
Cities should make their lights as bright as possible at all times.
Only scientists are able to see the stars at night.
There is nothing anyone can do about light pollution.

listeningQuestions.transcript: Would you mind turning down the music while I finish this phone call?
The train leaves platform seven at eight forty and arrives at half past ten.
Rarely have I seen a project completed so smoothly and so far ahead of schedule.
You should have told me you were coming so I could have saved you a seat.
The thin thread threaded through three thick things without tearing at all.
Please remember to switch off the lights before you lock up the office.
